pi_items.source: setup_task.sh: service_catalog.yaml contains 9 service definitions (api-gateway, customer-service, notification-service, payment-service, fraud-detection-service, ledger-service, checkout-service, order-service, reporting-service)
Scoring threshold defined in task.json and README.md success criteria. Represents a reasonable minimum (7 out of 9 = 78%) for partial credit on the audit task.

setup_task.sh comment: 'Adapted from: Google Online Boutique open-source reference (Apache 2.0)' and URL 'https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/microservices-demo'. Verified via web search: Online Boutique is a real, public Apache 2.0 licensed reference application with 11 microservices. Task adapts it for fintech context with 9 services (not a direct copy).

setup_task.sh: line 26 copies from /workspace/assets/diagrams/microservices_partial.drawio to /home/ga/Diagrams/. Source file verified to exist at benchmarks/cua_world/environments/draw_desktop_env/assets/diagrams/microservices_partial.drawio

privileged_info_summary: The task involves completing and correcting a microservices architecture diagram. The authoritative data source is a 9-service fintech microservices catalog (adapted from Google's Online Boutique project) with 3 domains (Customer, Payment, Operations). The partial starter diagram contains only 3 of 9 services with 2 marked wrong edges and 2 incorrect tech stacks. The correct topology has 12 directed edges representing dependencies. The task requires agents to audit the partial diagram against the catalog, fix errors, add 6 missing services, group by domains, label protocols on edges, and create a second dependency matrix page. Metadata thresholds (min 7 services, min 10 edges, min 2 pages) are all verified against the actual catalog data.
